findings.evidence: Current lines 55-58 use `∀ m ∈ P, coact m ∈ submoduleCoactionRange P`; Mathlib’s invariant-submodule API uses order/map forms (`Mathlib/Algebra/Module/Submodule/Invariant.lean:35-50`), and the tensor inclusion already has the standard name `P.subtype.rTensor C` (`Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/TensorProduct/Map.lean:331`, used in `Finiteness.lean:132`).
findings.fix: Define the predicate as `P.map (Comodule.coact (R := R) (C := C) (M := M)) ≤ LinearMap.range (P.subtype.rTensor C)` and keep the elementwise/existence form as an iff lemma if needed.
findings.issue: `IsSubcomodule` is defined elementwise using the bespoke `submoduleCoactionRange`, so users cannot use the standard `Submodule.map`/order API directly for stability.

summary: The stability condition is at the right mathematical scope, but the public predicate is exposed below the natural Mathlib submodule-map level.
